title: [EPIC] EP01: Multi-Tenant Infrastructure & Platform Foundation
body:
## Epic: Multi-Tenant Infrastructure & Platform Foundation

**Objective:** Establish the core multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure, authentication, billing, and control plane that all agents and features depend on.

**Phase:** 1 (Months 1-4)

**Key Outcomes:**
- AWS infrastructure provisioned (EKS, RDS, Redis, SQS, S3)
- Multi-tenant control plane with tenant isolation
- OAuth 2.0 flow for Amazon SP-API and Ads API authorization
- CI/CD pipeline with infrastructure-as-code
- Monitoring and observability stack deployed

**Dependencies:** None (foundational)

**Related Stories:** #2 through #12

title: [STORY] Tenant onboarding with Amazon SP-API OAuth authorization
body:
## User Story

**As a** new Amazon seller,
**I want to** connect my Amazon Seller Central account to the platform via OAuth,
**So that** the platform's AI agents can securely access my seller data without sharing my credentials.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Seller can initiate Login with Amazon (LWA) OAuth 2.0 flow from the onboarding screen
- [ ] Platform requests only the minimum required SP-API roles (Product Listing, Pricing, Inventory, etc.)
- [ ] OAuth refresh tokens are encrypted at rest with per-tenant encryption keys
- [ ] Seller can see which permissions they've granted and revoke access at any time
- [ ] Multi-marketplace authorization supported (NA, EU, FE regions)
- [ ] Onboarding wizard validates API connectivity before completing setup
- [ ] Tenant record created in control plane with unique tenant_id
- [ ] All subsequent API calls are scoped to the authorized seller's account

## Technical Notes
- Use Amazon's OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant flow
- Store refresh tokens in AWS Secrets Manager or encrypted DB column
- Implement token refresh logic with retry and exponential backoff
- Support both self-authorized (private) and OAuth-authorized (public) app models

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Multi-tenant database with row-level security isolation
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform operator,
**I want** every database query to be automatically scoped to the requesting tenant,
**So that** no seller can ever access another seller's data, even if application code has bugs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS) policies enforce tenant isolation on all tables
- [ ] Every table includes a `tenant_id` column as part of the RLS policy
- [ ] `tenant_id` is set by middleware from the authenticated session, never by AI agents or user input
- [ ] Per-tenant encryption keys for sensitive data (API tokens, financial data)
- [ ] Database migration system supports RLS policy updates
- [ ] Automated tests verify cross-tenant data access is impossible
- [ ] Connection pooling configured with tenant-aware session variables
- [ ] Query performance is not degraded by more than 5% due to RLS overhead

## Technical Notes
- Use `SET app.current_tenant = 'tenant_id'` on each connection
- RLS policies: `CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON table USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant'))`
- pgvector extension for semantic search per tenant
- Consider schema-per-tenant for enterprise tier customers

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Tenant billing and subscription management
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want to** choose a subscription plan and manage my billing,
**So that** I can access the automation features appropriate for my business size.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Subscription tiers available: Starter ($99/mo), Growth ($299/mo), Professional ($599/mo), Enterprise ($1,499+/mo)
- [ ] Stripe integration for payment processing and subscription management
- [ ] Usage-based billing for AI agent actions beyond tier allowances
- [ ] Ad spend commission tracking (2-5% of managed spend above tier limits)
- [ ] Self-service plan upgrades and downgrades
- [ ] Invoice generation and billing history dashboard
- [ ] Grace period and dunning for failed payments
- [ ] Feature flags tied to subscription tier (e.g., number of agents, marketplaces)

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] API gateway with rate limiting and tenant routing
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform operator,
**I want** all API requests to be authenticated, rate-limited per tenant, and routed to the correct agent services,
**So that** the platform remains stable and no single tenant can degrade service for others.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] API gateway authenticates all requests via JWT/session tokens (Amazon Cognito)
- [ ] Per-tenant rate limiting with configurable thresholds per subscription tier
- [ ] Request routing to appropriate agent microservices based on endpoint
- [ ] Noisy-neighbor protection: per-tenant resource quotas and fair scheduling
- [ ] API versioning support (v1, v2, etc.)
- [ ] Request/response logging with tenant context for debugging
- [ ] Health check endpoints for all services
- [ ] Circuit breaker pattern for downstream Amazon API failures

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Event-driven architecture with SQS and EventBridge
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform developer,
**I want** an event bus that routes Amazon SP-API notifications and internal agent events to the correct tenant's agent pipeline,
**So that** agents can react to real-time changes (price updates, inventory events, listing issues) asynchronously.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SQS FIFO queues for ordered per-tenant event processing
- [ ] EventBridge rules for cross-service event routing
- [ ] SP-API notification subscriptions: ANY_OFFER_CHANGED, LISTINGS_ITEM_STATUS_CHANGE, LISTINGS_ITEM_ISSUES_CHANGE, ITEM_INVENTORY_EVENT_CHANGE, ITEM_SALES_EVENT_CHANGE, ORDER_CHANGE
- [ ] Dead letter queues for failed event processing
- [ ] Event schema registry for type-safe event contracts
- [ ] Per-tenant event filtering and routing
- [ ] Retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures
- [ ] Event replay capability for debugging

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] CI/CD pipeline and infrastructure-as-code
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform developer,
**I want** automated deployment pipelines and IaC for all infrastructure,
**So that** we can deploy reliably, reproduce environments, and scale without manual configuration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Terraform or AWS CDK for all infrastructure provisioning
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for automated testing and deployment
- [ ] Separate environments: dev, staging, production
- [ ] Container image builds with security scanning
- [ ] Database migration automation with rollback capability
- [ ] Feature flag management (LaunchDarkly or similar)
- [ ] Automated integration tests against Amazon SP-API sandbox
- [ ] Infrastructure drift detection

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Monitoring, observability, and agent tracing
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform operator,
**I want** comprehensive monitoring of infrastructure health, API performance, and AI agent behavior,
**So that** I can detect issues proactively and debug agent decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CloudWatch metrics and alarms for all AWS resources
- [ ] Datadog APM for request tracing across microservices
- [ ] LangSmith integration for LLM call tracing and agent observability
- [ ] Per-tenant dashboards showing API usage, agent actions, and error rates
- [ ] Alert thresholds: API latency > 2s, error rate > 1%, agent failure rate > 5%
- [ ] Decision trace logs: every agent decision logged with prompt, context, reasoning, and outcome
- [ ] Cost tracking per tenant for LLM API usage
- [ ] System uptime target: 99.9%

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Human approval workflow engine
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** AI agents to propose actions and wait for my approval before executing high-impact changes,
**So that** I maintain control over my business while benefiting from AI automation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Configurable approval policies: which actions require human approval vs auto-execute
- [ ] Approval queue in dashboard with clear descriptions of proposed actions
- [ ] One-click approve/reject with optional feedback
- [ ] Approval timeout with configurable auto-reject/auto-approve behavior
- [ ] Batch approval for similar actions (e.g., approve all price changes within 5%)
- [ ] Approval delegation to team members with role-based permissions
- [ ] Push notifications (email, mobile) for pending approvals
- [ ] Full audit trail of all approvals, rejections, and auto-executions
- [ ] Gradual autonomy: sellers can increase agent autonomy as trust builds

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Seller dashboard with real-time agent activity feed
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a real-time dashboard showing what my AI agents are doing, what they've accomplished, and what needs my attention,
**So that** I have full visibility into automated operations without manually checking each function.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Real-time activity feed showing agent actions via WebSocket updates
- [ ] Summary cards: listings managed, orders processed, ad spend, revenue, profit
- [ ] Pending approval queue prominently displayed
- [ ] Agent status indicators: active, idle, error, awaiting approval
- [ ] Quick filters by agent type (listing, pricing, ads, inventory)
- [ ] Time-range selector for historical activity
- [ ] Responsive design for desktop and tablet
- [ ] Per-marketplace view toggle for multi-marketplace sellers

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP02: Product Listing Automation
body:
## Epic: Product Listing Automation

**Objective:** Build an AI agent that automates product listing creation, optimization, and maintenance using Amazon SP-API.

**Phase:** 1 (Months 3-4)

**Key Outcomes:**
- AI-generated SEO-optimized product titles, bullet points, and descriptions
- Automated listing quality monitoring and issue resolution
- Product type definition lookups and attribute mapping
- Listing suppression detection and remediation

**APIs:** Listings Items API, Catalog Items API, Product Type Definitions API, Feeds API, Notifications API

**Related Stories:** #11 through #16

title: [STORY] AI-powered product listing generation
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to generate SEO-optimized product titles, bullet points, and descriptions from minimal input,
**So that** I can list new products in minutes instead of hours, with content optimized for Amazon's A10 algorithm.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Seller provides: product name, key features, images, and target keywords
- [ ] Agent generates: title (≤200 chars), 5 bullet points, description, and backend search terms
- [ ] Content follows Amazon's style guidelines and category-specific requirements
- [ ] Keywords naturally integrated without keyword stuffing
- [ ] Competitor analysis informs content strategy (top 10 ASINs in category analyzed)
- [ ] Seller can configure brand voice preferences (professional, casual, technical)
- [ ] Generated content presented for human review before submission
- [ ] Agent uses Product Type Definitions API to ensure all required attributes are populated
- [ ] Support for variation listings (parent-child relationships)

## Technical Notes
- Use Claude API for content generation with category-specific system prompts
- Integrate Catalog Items API to analyze competitor listings
- Use Product Type Definitions API for schema validation before submission
- Submit via Listings Items API (putListingsItem)

## Epic: EP02 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Listing quality monitoring and auto-remediation
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to continuously monitor my listing quality scores and automatically fix issues,
**So that** my listings never get suppressed or lose search visibility due to quality problems.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Subscribe to LISTINGS_ITEM_ISSUES_CHANGE and LISTINGS_ITEM_STATUS_CHANGE notifications
- [ ] Detect and categorize issues: missing attributes, image quality, suppression risks, policy violations
- [ ] Auto-fix low-risk issues (missing backend keywords, incomplete attributes) without human approval
- [ ] Queue high-risk fixes (title changes, main image changes) for human approval
- [ ] Dashboard showing listing health scores per ASIN with trend indicators
- [ ] Alert seller when listings are suppressed or at risk of suppression
- [ ] Track listing quality improvements over time
- [ ] Integration with Amazon's Listing Quality Dashboard recommendations

## Epic: EP02 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Bulk listing creation and CSV import
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller with a large catalog,
**I want to** upload a CSV/spreadsheet of products and have the AI agent generate optimized listings for all of them,
**So that** I can onboard my entire catalog efficiently.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Accept CSV/XLSX upload with product attributes (name, features, category, keywords)
- [ ] AI generates optimized content for each product in batch
- [ ] Progress indicator showing batch processing status
- [ ] Review interface for batch approving/editing generated listings
- [ ] Submit via JSON Listings Feed for bulk operations
- [ ] Error handling with per-item status reporting
- [ ] Rate limit compliance for batch API calls

## Epic: EP02 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Listing content A/B testing
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to run A/B tests on my listing titles and bullet points,
**So that** I can continuously improve conversion rates with data-driven content optimization.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent generates 2-3 content variants per listing element
- [ ] Automated rotation of variants over configurable test periods
- [ ] Statistical significance calculation before declaring a winner
- [ ] Auto-promotion of winning variants (with seller approval)
- [ ] Test results dashboard with conversion rate, CTR, and session data per variant
- [ ] Integration with Data Kiosk API for sales and traffic metrics

## Epic: EP02 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP03: A+ Content & Brand Store Management
body:
## Epic: A+ Content & Brand Store Management

**Objective:** AI agent that creates and manages A+ Content and Brand Store pages programmatically.

**Phase:** 3 (Months 9-10)

**Key Outcomes:**
- AI-generated A+ Content modules (comparison charts, brand stories, enhanced images)
- Programmatic Brand Store management via Brand Stores Management API (GA Feb 2026)
- Section-level engagement tracking and optimization
- A/B testing for content performance

**APIs:** A+ Content Management API, Brand Stores Management API, Stores Analytics API

**Key Stat:** Amazon's data shows high-quality stores generate up to 97% more sales than low-quality ones.

title: [STORY] AI-generated A+ Content modules
body:
## User Story

**As a** brand-registered seller,
**I want** the AI agent to generate A+ Content modules including comparison charts, brand stories, and enhanced product descriptions,
**So that** my detail pages convert better and reflect my brand identity.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent generates A+ Content modules based on product data and brand guidelines
- [ ] Supported module types: comparison charts, standard text + image, brand story, feature highlights
- [ ] Brand voice and visual style configurable per tenant
- [ ] Preview rendering before submission to Amazon
- [ ] Submit via A+ Content Management API with moderation handling
- [ ] Track content approval status and resubmit if rejected with corrections
- [ ] Performance tracking: conversion rate lift from A+ content vs standard listings

## Epic: EP03 | Priority: P1 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Brand Store programmatic management
body:
## User Story

**As a** brand-registered seller,
**I want** the AI agent to manage my Amazon Brand Store pages programmatically,
**So that** my storefront stays current with new products, seasonal themes, and optimized layouts without manual updates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Retrieve and update Brand Store pages via Brand Stores Management API
- [ ] Auto-add new products to relevant store categories
- [ ] Seasonal layout recommendations based on shopping calendar
- [ ] Section-level engagement metrics via Stores Analytics API
- [ ] Submit store updates for moderation with status tracking
- [ ] Store quality scoring aligned with Amazon's quality tiers

## Epic: EP03 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP04: Inventory Management & Demand Forecasting
body:
## Epic: Inventory Management & Demand Forecasting

**Objective:** AI agent that monitors inventory levels, forecasts demand, and automates replenishment.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 5-6)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Real-time inventory monitoring across FBA, FBM, and AWD
- ML-powered demand forecasting with seasonality awareness
- Automated replenishment plan generation
- Cross-agent coordination (reduce ads when stock is low)

**APIs:** FBA Inventory API, AWD API, Fulfillment Inbound API, Replenishment API, Notifications API

title: [STORY] Real-time inventory monitoring and alerts
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to monitor my inventory levels in real-time across all fulfillment channels,
**So that** I never run out of stock unexpectedly or have excess inventory tying up capital.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Real-time inventory data via FBA Inventory API and ITEM_INVENTORY_EVENT_CHANGE notifications
- [ ] Dashboard showing inventory levels per ASIN, per fulfillment center
- [ ] Configurable low-stock and overstock alert thresholds
- [ ] Days-of-stock calculation based on current sell-through rate
- [ ] Automatic notifications when stock reaches reorder point
- [ ] Cross-agent event: publish LOW_STOCK event to advertising and pricing agents
- [ ] Support for FBA, FBM, and AWD inventory tracking
- [ ] Stranded inventory detection and resolution suggestions

## Epic: EP04 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] AI demand forecasting and replenishment planning
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to predict future demand and automatically generate replenishment plans,
**So that** I maintain optimal stock levels without manual calculation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Demand forecasting using historical sales data, seasonality, and trend analysis
- [ ] Factor in upcoming promotions, advertising changes, and market events
- [ ] Generate purchase order recommendations with quantities and timing
- [ ] Auto-create inbound shipment plans to FBA/AWD via Fulfillment Inbound API
- [ ] Lead time awareness per supplier
- [ ] Configurable safety stock levels
- [ ] Forecast accuracy tracking and model improvement over time
- [ ] Slow-moving inventory detection with liquidation/removal recommendations

## Epic: EP04 | Priority: P1 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP05: PPC Advertising Automation (SP + SB)
body:
## Epic: PPC Advertising Automation

**Objective:** AI agent that creates, manages, and optimizes Amazon PPC campaigns using the Ads MCP Server and Advertising API.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 5-6)

**Key Outcomes:**
- End-to-end campaign creation via MCP natural-language prompts
- Automated bid optimization based on ACoS/ROAS/TACoS targets
- Keyword harvesting from Auto to Manual campaigns
- Cross-agent coordination with inventory and pricing
- Multi-marketplace campaign expansion

**APIs:** Amazon Ads API (Campaign Management v3), Amazon Ads MCP Server, AMC, Marketing Stream, Unified Reporting API

**Key Integration:** Amazon Ads MCP Server (open beta Feb 2026) reduces 3+ manual operations to single prompts.

title: [STORY] Campaign creation via Amazon Ads MCP Server
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to create complete Sponsored Products and Sponsored Brands campaigns via natural-language prompts,
**So that** I can launch campaigns in seconds instead of spending 15-20 minutes per campaign in Campaign Manager.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Connect to Amazon Ads MCP Server with seller's API credentials
- [ ] Create end-to-end SP campaigns (campaign + ad group + ads) via single MCP tool call
- [ ] Create SB campaigns with brand store landing pages
- [ ] Support automatic and manual targeting types
- [ ] Set initial bids based on category benchmarks and seller's historical data
- [ ] Budget allocation based on seller's total advertising budget and product priorities
- [ ] Campaign creation requires seller approval before going live
- [ ] Support campaign expansion to new marketplaces via MCP expansion tools
- [ ] Error handling for MCP Server failures with graceful fallback to direct API

## Technical Notes
- MCP tools: cp_createCampaign, cp_updateCampaign, cp_listCampaigns
- Ads MCP Server handles multi-step orchestration internally
- SP data lookback: 95 days; SB/SD data lookback: 60 days
- MCP Server has NO inventory visibility — must coordinate with Inventory Agent

## Epic: EP05 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Automated bid optimization and keyword management
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to continuously optimize my bids and manage keywords based on performance data,
**So that** my advertising spend generates maximum ROI without constant manual adjustment.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Real-time bid adjustment based on ACoS, ROAS, and TACoS targets
- [ ] Keyword harvesting: extract converting search terms from Auto campaigns, create Manual campaigns
- [ ] Negative keyword management: identify and add wasteful search terms as negatives
- [ ] Dayparting support: adjust bids by time of day based on conversion patterns
- [ ] Budget pacing: prevent overspend early in the day, reallocate to high-performing campaigns
- [ ] Cross-agent signal: reduce bids when inventory agent reports low stock on an ASIN
- [ ] Cross-agent signal: adjust bids when pricing agent reports non-competitive price
- [ ] Performance reports via Amazon Marketing Stream for near-real-time data
- [ ] Configurable optimization aggressiveness (conservative, balanced, aggressive)

## Epic: EP05 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Advertising performance reporting and analytics
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** comprehensive advertising performance reports with actionable AI-generated insights,
**So that** I can understand what's working, what's not, and make informed strategic decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Dashboard with key metrics: spend, sales, ACoS, ROAS, TACoS, impressions, clicks, CTR, CPC
- [ ] Campaign, ad group, keyword, and ASIN-level drill-down
- [ ] AI-generated weekly performance summary with recommendations
- [ ] Conversion path reporting using AMC data (30-day attribution window)
- [ ] Unified reporting across SP, SB, and SD campaigns
- [ ] Trend analysis with period-over-period comparison
- [ ] Exportable reports (CSV, PDF)
- [ ] Scheduled report delivery via email

## Epic: EP05 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP06: Dynamic Pricing & Buy Box Optimization
body:
## Epic: Dynamic Pricing & Buy Box Optimization

**Objective:** AI agent that implements intelligent, real-time pricing strategies to win the Buy Box while maintaining target margins.

**Phase:** 1 (Months 3-4)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Real-time competitor price monitoring
- Game-theory pricing to win Buy Box without price wars
- Margin-aware pricing factoring in all fees and costs
- Cross-agent coordination with advertising and inventory

**APIs:** Product Pricing API, Notifications API (ANY_OFFER_CHANGED), Feeds API, Product Fees API

title: [STORY] Real-time competitor price monitoring and repricing
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to monitor competitor prices in real-time and adjust my prices to stay competitive,
**So that** I win the Buy Box more often while maintaining my target profit margins.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Subscribe to ANY_OFFER_CHANGED and B2B_ANY_OFFER_CHANGED notifications for all seller ASINs
- [ ] Track competitor prices, fulfillment types, and seller ratings
- [ ] Apply game-theory pricing strategy to avoid destructive price wars
- [ ] Calculate true cost per unit: product cost + FBA fees + shipping + advertising + referral fee
- [ ] Enforce seller-configured minimum and maximum price boundaries
- [ ] Reprice within configurable response time (default: < 5 minutes from competitor change)
- [ ] Buy Box win rate tracking per ASIN
- [ ] Surge pricing during high-demand periods
- [ ] Markdown pricing for slow-moving or excess inventory
- [ ] Cross-agent signal: pause ad spend when price is not competitive for Buy Box

## Technical Notes
- Use Product Pricing API for getItemOffers and getListingOffersBatch
- Submit price changes via Feeds API (price feed) or Listings Items API (patchListingsItem)
- Product Fees API for real-time fee calculations

## Epic: EP06 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP07: Competitor Analysis & Market Intelligence
body:
## Epic: Competitor Analysis & Market Intelligence

**Objective:** AI agent that tracks competitors, analyzes market dynamics, and generates strategic intelligence.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 7-8)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Competitor ASIN tracking (pricing, reviews, BSR, content changes)
- Brand Analytics integration (search frequency rank, click share, conversion share)
- New market entrant detection
- Competitive landscape reports with actionable recommendations

**APIs:** Catalog Items API, Product Pricing API, Data Kiosk API (Brand Analytics), Reports API

title: [STORY] Competitor ASIN tracking and change detection
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to track my competitors' ASINs and alert me to significant changes,
**So that** I can react quickly to competitor pricing changes, new reviews, listing updates, and BSR movements.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Seller can add competitor ASINs to watchlist (manual or AI-suggested)
- [ ] Track: price changes, review count/rating changes, listing content modifications, BSR changes
- [ ] Configurable alert thresholds (e.g., alert when competitor drops price by >10%)
- [ ] AI-generated competitive response recommendations
- [ ] Daily/weekly competitive landscape digest
- [ ] New market entrant detection in seller's categories
- [ ] Competitor ad placement monitoring (estimated share of voice)

## Epic: EP07 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Brand Analytics integration for market intelligence
body:
## User Story

**As a** brand-registered seller,
**I want** the AI agent to analyze Brand Analytics data to identify keyword opportunities and market share trends,
**So that** I can make data-driven decisions about product positioning and advertising strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Integrate Data Kiosk API with Brand Analytics search terms schema
- [ ] Track search frequency rank for target keywords over time
- [ ] Monitor click share and conversion share vs competitors
- [ ] Identify high-opportunity keywords: high search volume + low competition
- [ ] Feed keyword intelligence to Listing Agent and Advertising Agent
- [ ] Market share estimation per category/subcategory
- [ ] Trend analysis: rising and declining search terms

## Epic: EP07 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP08: Keyword Research & SEO Optimization
body:
## Epic: Keyword Research & SEO Optimization

**Objective:** AI agent that performs comprehensive keyword research and continuously optimizes listing SEO.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 7-8)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Reverse ASIN keyword extraction from competitors
- Brand Analytics search term analysis
- Keyword gap identification and prioritization
- Continuous listing SEO optimization

**APIs:** Data Kiosk API (Brand Analytics Search Terms), Catalog Items API, Advertising Keyword Recommendations API

title: [STORY] Reverse ASIN keyword research and gap analysis
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to extract high-performing keywords from competitor listings and identify gaps in my keyword strategy,
**So that** my listings rank for all relevant search terms customers use.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Extract indexed keywords from top 10 competitor ASINs per product
- [ ] Compare seller's indexed keywords against competitor keyword profiles
- [ ] Identify keyword gaps: terms competitors rank for but seller doesn't
- [ ] Prioritize keywords by search volume, relevance, and competition level
- [ ] Generate keyword integration recommendations for listing content
- [ ] Auto-update backend search terms with high-priority missing keywords
- [ ] Feed keyword data to Advertising Agent for campaign targeting
- [ ] Weekly keyword performance report with ranking changes

## Epic: EP08 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP09: Promotions & Deals Management
body:
## Epic: Promotions & Deals Management

**Objective:** AI agent that creates and optimizes promotional campaigns, deals, and coupons.

**Phase:** 3 (Months 11-12)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Lightning Deals, Best Deals, and Coupon management
- Promotional calendar optimization
- ROI tracking including halo effects on organic ranking
- Coordination with inventory for deal stock requirements

**APIs:** Promotions API, Coupons API, Feeds API

title: [STORY] Automated deal creation and promotional calendar
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to recommend and create optimal promotional deals based on my inventory, margin, and market data,
**So that** I can boost sales velocity and organic ranking at the right times with the right discounts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent analyzes historical deal performance and category seasonality
- [ ] Recommends deal type (Lightning Deal, Best Deal, Coupon), timing, and discount depth
- [ ] Coordinates with Inventory Agent to verify sufficient stock for promotional period
- [ ] Coordinates with Pricing Agent to set optimal pre-deal and post-deal prices
- [ ] Creates deals programmatically with seller approval
- [ ] Tracks promotional ROI: direct sales lift + halo effect on organic ranking + review velocity
- [ ] Promotional calendar view showing planned and active deals
- [ ] Budget tracking and alerts for promotional spend

## Epic: EP09 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP10: Order Management & Customer Service
body:
## Epic: Order Management & Customer Service

**Objective:** AI agent that automates order processing, customer communications, and review solicitation.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 7-8)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Automated order confirmation and tracking updates
- AI-drafted customer messages within Amazon's guidelines
- Review solicitation at optimal timing
- Account health monitoring for order-related metrics

**APIs:** Orders API, Messaging API, Solicitations API, Customer Feedback API

title: [STORY] Automated order processing and customer messaging
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to handle routine order processing and customer communications automatically,
**So that** I meet Amazon's response time requirements and maintain high customer satisfaction without manual effort.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Subscribe to ORDER_CHANGE notifications for real-time order updates
- [ ] Auto-confirm shipments and update tracking information
- [ ] AI-draft customer messages for common scenarios (shipping delays, order issues)
- [ ] Messages sent via Messaging API within Amazon's communication guidelines
- [ ] Configurable auto-response rules vs human review requirements
- [ ] Auto-process returns/refunds below configurable threshold (e.g., < $25)
- [ ] Escalate complex issues (A-to-Z claims, high-value disputes) to human
- [ ] Solicit reviews via Solicitations API at optimal timing (post-delivery + satisfaction signal)
- [ ] Customer satisfaction metrics tracking

## Epic: EP10 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP11: Financial Analytics & Profitability Tracking
body:
## Epic: Financial Analytics & Profitability Tracking

**Objective:** AI agent that provides real-time profitability analysis and financial intelligence.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 7-8)

**Key Outcomes:**
- SKU-level true profitability (including all fees, ads, returns)
- TACoS tracking and optimization
- Automated P&L reports per product, brand, and marketplace
- Cash flow projection and reorder budget recommendations

**APIs:** Finances API, Reports API, Product Fees API, Data Kiosk API

title: [STORY] SKU-level profitability dashboard
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** to see true profit/loss for every SKU factoring in all costs,
**So that** I can identify which products are making money and which are draining resources.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Calculate per-SKU profitability: revenue - COGS - FBA fees - referral fees - ad spend - returns - shipping
- [ ] Real-time financial data via Finances API and ITEM_SALES_EVENT_CHANGE notifications
- [ ] TACoS (Total Advertising Cost of Sale) calculation per SKU
- [ ] Automated P&L reports: daily, weekly, monthly per product/brand/marketplace
- [ ] Identify unprofitable SKUs with AI-generated recommendations (price increase, cut ads, discontinue)
- [ ] Cash flow projection based on sales velocity, pending settlements, and planned purchases
- [ ] Fee change impact analysis (alert when Amazon changes fee structure)
- [ ] Export to CSV/Excel and integration with accounting software

## Epic: EP11 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP12: Compliance & Account Health Monitoring
body:
## Epic: Compliance & Account Health Monitoring

**Objective:** AI agent that proactively monitors account health, policy compliance, and regulatory requirements.

**Phase:** 3 (Months 9-10)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Continuous account health monitoring (ODR, late shipment rate, etc.)
- Product safety documentation compliance
- Multi-marketplace regulatory compliance (EPR, VAT, etc.)
- Proactive issue detection and resolution

**APIs:** Sellers API, Reports API, Notifications API, Listings Restrictions API

title: [STORY] Proactive account health monitoring and alerts
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to continuously monitor my account health metrics and proactively resolve issues,
**So that** my account is never at risk of suspension due to performance problems.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Monitor Order Defect Rate (ODR), Late Shipment Rate, Pre-Fulfillment Cancellation Rate
- [ ] Alert when any metric approaches warning threshold (before Amazon flags it)
- [ ] Product safety documentation compliance checking
- [ ] Listing restriction checks via Listings Restrictions API before new product listings
- [ ] Auto-ensure products meet compliance in all selling countries
- [ ] EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) declaration management for EU markets
- [ ] Proactive IP complaint detection and response recommendations
- [ ] Account health trend dashboard with risk scoring

## Epic: EP12 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP13: Multi-Marketplace Expansion
body:
## Epic: Multi-Marketplace Expansion

**Objective:** Enable sellers to expand to new Amazon marketplaces with AI-assisted localization.

**Phase:** 3 (Months 11-12)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Market demand analysis across NA, EU, FE regions
- AI-powered listing translation with cultural localization
- Cross-marketplace ad campaign replication via MCP
- Per-marketplace performance monitoring

**APIs:** All SP-APIs (multi-region), Ads MCP Server (region expansion tools)

title: [STORY] AI-powered marketplace expansion with localized listings
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the AI agent to help me expand to new Amazon marketplaces with properly localized listings and campaigns,
**So that** I can grow my business internationally without hiring native-language specialists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Market demand analysis for seller's product categories across target marketplaces
- [ ] AI-powered listing translation with cultural localization (not just word-for-word)
- [ ] Marketplace-specific compliance checks (VAT, product regulations, labeling)
- [ ] Currency conversion and pricing strategy per marketplace
- [ ] Replicate successful ad campaigns to new marketplaces via MCP expansion tools
- [ ] Per-marketplace performance dashboards
- [ ] Unified cross-marketplace view for sellers operating in multiple regions

## Epic: EP13 | Priority: P2 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP14: Product Launch Orchestration
body:
## Epic: Product Launch Orchestration

**Objective:** Coordinate all agents for a seamless, automated product launch sequence.

**Phase:** 3 (Month 12)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Coordinated multi-agent launch sequence
- Launch velocity monitoring and dynamic optimization
- Post-launch analysis and recommendations

This is the ultimate demonstration of cross-agent coordination — the feature that no point solution can provide.

title: [STORY] Coordinated multi-agent product launch sequence
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller launching a new product,
**I want** the AI orchestrator to coordinate all agents in a synchronized launch sequence,
**So that** my product launch is optimized across listing, pricing, advertising, and inventory from day one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Launch workflow: Listing Agent creates optimized listing → Inventory Agent verifies stock → Pricing Agent sets launch price → Advertising Agent activates PPC campaigns → Promotions Agent schedules launch deals
- [ ] Configurable launch timeline with pre-launch, launch, and post-launch phases
- [ ] Real-time launch velocity monitoring (orders/day, BSR trajectory, review acquisition)
- [ ] Dynamic adjustment: increase ad spend if velocity is below target, decrease if stock is running low
- [ ] Post-launch analysis report: what worked, what to optimize, projected trajectory
- [ ] Launch playbook templates for different product types and categories
- [ ] Human approval gates at each phase transition

## Epic: EP14 | Priority: P2 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Orchestrator agent for cross-domain coordination
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform,
**I want** a central orchestrator agent that coordinates workflows across all specialist agents,
**So that** business decisions account for cross-domain context (e.g., inventory affects pricing affects advertising).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator receives events from all specialist agents via event bus
- [ ] Implements cross-agent workflows: low stock → reduce ads + increase price
- [ ] Resolves conflicts between agent recommendations (e.g., pricing agent wants lower price, margin agent wants higher)
- [ ] Manages human approval queue: prioritizes, deduplicates, and batches approval requests
- [ ] Maintains per-tenant strategy context: seller's goals, risk tolerance, growth vs profit mode
- [ ] Decision audit trail: log every orchestration decision with reasoning
- [ ] Fallback handling: graceful degradation if a specialist agent is unavailable
- [ ] Configurable orchestration rules per tenant

## Epic: EP05/Cross-cutting | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Amazon Ads API credential management and MCP connection
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** to connect my Amazon Advertising account to the platform,
**So that** the advertising agent can manage my PPC campaigns.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OAuth flow for Amazon Ads API authorization
- [ ] Secure storage of Ads API refresh tokens (per-tenant encrypted)
- [ ] Advertiser profile discovery and selection (support multiple profiles/brands)
- [ ] MCP Server connection setup with seller's credentials
- [ ] Region auto-routing (NA, EU, FE)
- [ ] Connection health monitoring and token refresh management
- [ ] Graceful handling when MCP Server is unavailable (fallback to direct API)

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P0 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Seller preference and strategy configuration
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** to configure my business goals, risk tolerance, and agent behavior preferences,
**So that** the AI agents make decisions aligned with my strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Business mode selector: growth mode (maximize revenue) vs profit mode (maximize margins)
- [ ] Per-agent autonomy levels: full auto, auto with notifications, approval required, disabled
- [ ] Pricing preferences: min/max margins, MAP compliance, competitor response aggressiveness
- [ ] Advertising preferences: target ACoS/ROAS, daily budget limits, marketplace priorities
- [ ] Brand voice configuration for listing content generation
- [ ] Notification preferences: email, in-app, frequency
- [ ] Settings apply across all agents via shared tenant configuration

## Epic: EP01 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5
